sentences.quote: The single substantive axiom in the chain (\texttt{FirstOrderKMS}) was reformulated after Aristotle's counterexample $c = (0,0,0,0,0,0,0,1,0)$ exposed that the original axiom constrained only four of nine field components

The phrase 'the single substantive axiom in the chain (FirstOrderKMS)' is structurally inaccurate: (a) FirstOrderKMS is a Prop, not an axiom; (b) the project's only Lean axiom is gapped_interface_axiom (D2/D4 territory). The Aristotle-counterexample story for FirstOrderKMS is correct, but the framing 'single substantive axiom' is wrong. Recommend: 'The load-bearing structural Prop in the chain (FirstOrderKMS) was reformulated …' or 'a load-bearing tracked-hypothesis-style Prop in the chain'.

sentences.section: Exact WKB connection: three non-perturbative effects
sentences.quote: For Heidelberg-class BEC ($\delta_\text{diss} \approx 10^{-3}$), $\omega_\times \approx 6\, T_H$
sentences.quote_normalized: for heidelberg-class bec ($\delta_diss \approx 10^{-3}$), $\omega_\times \approx 6\, t_h$
sentences.agent_notes: IA BLOCKER: §3.5 states Heidelberg-class delta_diss ≈ 10^-3 = 0.1%; Table 1 (line 452) reports Heidelberg delta_diss ~ 26% — different by FOUR orders of magnitude on the same device. Either (a) §3.5 means a different platform (Steinhauer has delta_disp -2.7%, delta_diss 1e-5; closer to '10^-3'? still off), or (b) §3.5 should be re-anchored to a regime where delta_diss ~ 1e-3 is correct. Note also: with delta_diss = 1e-3, omega_x = T_H · ln(2/1e-3) = 7.6 T_H, not '~6 T_H'. With delta_diss = 1e-2, omega_x = 5.3 T_H ≈ 6 T_H (closer). Recommend a regime self-consistent triple (platform name, delta_diss, omega_x) be established and used uniformly. Cross-bundle: this affects D1 §3.5 abstract claim 'spectral floor at ~6 T_H' which only Heidelberg is supposed to access (line 426).

The structure FirstOrderKMS_altSign (line 610 of SKDoubling.lean) IS the post-counterexample alt-sign instantiation; line 649 contains the literal counterexample tuple ⟨1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 2, 0⟩. summary.agent_text: 22 high-priority sentences sampled (not full sentence-walk; bundle Tier-1 first-pass focused on anchor list + load-bearing claims). 5 PASS, 9 FAIL, 5 WARN, 1 INFO, 1 UNGROUNDED. 8 BLOCKERs: TN module-prefix drift (acoustic backbone, KMS optimal x2, graphene routes x3, lorenz-cancellation), IA Heidelberg delta_diss self-contradiction (10^-3 vs 26%), IA polariton smooth disp (10% vs 19%), IA EXPERIMENTS['BEC_KIM_2014'] missing key, Citation 4 missing WKB bibkeys + 1 missing CorleyJacobson1996 inline. Verdict: RED.

blocking_issues.summary: Class TN module-prefix drift: 4 of 5 acoustic-metric backbone theorem names wrong (metric_determinant, lorentzian_signature, inverse_identity, galilean_invariance — galilean_invariance does not exist anywhere in project)
Class TN: §3.3 SecondOrderSK.firstOrder_KMS_optimal and SecondOrderSK.firstOrder_altSign_uniqueness_test wrong module — actual SKDoubling.*
Class TN: GrapheneNoiseFormula.fdt_route, landauer_buttiker_route, routes_agree do not exist (closest extant: fdt_consistency)
Class TN: §6.7 DiracFluidSK.snr_independent_of_sigma_Q — actual is GrapheneNoiseFormula.snr_independent_of_sigma_Q (wrong module prefix)
Class TN: §2.2 FirstOrderKMS.gamma_nonnegativity does not exist (FirstOrderKMS is a structure with fields fdr_i1, fdr_i2, i3_zero, etc. — no field 'gamma_nonnegativity')
Class IA: Heidelberg delta_diss = 10^-3 in §3.5 contradicts hierarchy table value ~26% — four orders of magnitude. Spectral-floor claim 'omega_x ~ 6 T_H' depends on this.
Class IA: abstract says LKB polariton smooth-horizon delta_disp '~10%'; Table 1 reports -19% — internal inconsistency.

Class IA / parameter provenance: §2.3 references EXPERIMENTS['BEC_KIM_2014'] which is not in src/core/constants.py::EXPERIMENTS (existing keys: Steinhauer, Heidelberg, Trento). Bundle anchor §D1 #1 calls this load-bearing — anchor itself stale or registry entry missing.

non_blocking_followups.summary: Class Citation: 4 missing bibkeys (CorleyJacobson1996, CoutantParentani2012, RobertsonParentani2015, BelgiornoCacciatori2024) — \bibitem entries exist locally in D1 but CITATION_REGISTRY uses Corley1996 and CoutantParentani2010 as canonical. Validate.py CHECK 1 will fail.

Class Citation: 8 in-prep self-citations (Roehm2026Wave2/Wave12/Wave16/E1/E2/F/I1/I2) not in CITATION_REGISTRY but present as \bibitem — needs registry in_prep flag or bundle-source-manifest absorption rule.

Class SD: 92% Lean-reuse claim arithmetically rounds 109/119 = 91.6% — 'precise 92%' wording is mildly self-defeating. Phase 5w cross-platform reuse counter not directly cited as artifact (script path or stable-counter file).

Class TN/IA hybrid: abstract calls FirstOrderKMS the 'single substantive axiom' — but counts.json lists only 1 axiom (gapped_interface_axiom in D2/D4 territory) and FirstOrderKMS is a Prop structure, not an axiom. Reframe as 'load-bearing tracked-Prop' or similar.

Class TN cross-paper: abstract reference to firstOrder_KMS_optimal works as bare token (resolves in SKDoubling) but body §3.3 puts it under SecondOrderSK — the abstract version is fine, the body version is a BLOCKER. Together they show inconsistent module-prefixing within the same paper.

Reproducibility: hierarchy Table 1 caption says 'Numerical values are computed via src/core/formulas.py on EXPERIMENTS parameters' but the BEC_KIM_2014 key is missing and reviewer cannot recompute the table values in time-budgeted pass. Recommend a reproducibility hash (commit SHA + formulas.py version) emitted into the table caption.
